author: The Incarnation of the Word
book: On the Incarnation of the Word.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: On the Incarnation of the Word.

Why the Cross, of all deaths? (1) He had to bear the curse for us. (2) On it He held out His hands to unite all, Jews and Gentiles, in Himself. (3) He defeated the “Prince of the powers of the air” in His own region, clearing the way to heaven and opening for us the everlasting doors.

ccel.title: NPNF2-04. Athanasius: Select Works and Letters

source.title: NPNF2-04. Athanasius: Select Works and Letters

title: Why the Cross, of all deaths? (1) He had to bear the curse for us. (2) On it He held out His hands to unite all, Jews and Gentiles, in Himself. (3) He defeated the “Prince of the powers of the air” in His own region, clearing the way to heaven and opening for us the everlasting doors.

source.title: NPNF2-04. Athanasius: Select Works and Letters

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Why the Cross, of all deaths?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: (1) He had to bear the curse for us.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: (2) On it He held out His hands to unite all, Jews and Gentiles, in Himself.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: (3) He defeated the “Prince of the powers of the air” in His own region, clearing the way to heaven and opening for us the everlasting doors.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: And thus much in reply to those without who pile up arguments for themselves.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But if any of our own people also inquire, not from love of debate, but from love of learning, why He suffered death in none other way save on the Cross, let him also be told that no other way than this was good for us, and that it was well that the Lord suffered this for our sakes.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For if He came Himself to bear the curse laid upon us, how else could He have “become a curse,” unless He received the death set for a curse? and that is the Cross.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For this is exactly what is written: “Cursed is he that hangeth on a tree.”

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Again, if the Lord’s death is the ransom of all, and by His death “the middle wall of partition” is broken down, and the calling of the nations is brought about, how would He have called us to Him, had He not been crucified?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For it is only on the cross that a man dies with his hands spread out.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Whence it was fitting for the Lord to bear this also and to spread out His hands, that with the one He might draw the ancient people, and with the other those from the Gentiles, and unite both in Himself.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For this is what He Himself has said, signifying by what manner of death He was to ransom all: “I, when I am lifted up,” He saith, “shall draw all men unto Me.”

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: And once more, if the devil, the enemy of our race, having fallen from heaven, wanders about our lower atmosphere, and there bearing rule over his fellow-spirits, as his peers in disobedience, not only works illusions by their means in them that are deceived, but tries to hinder them that are going up (and about this the Apostle says: “According to the prince of the power of the air, of the spirit that now worketh in the sons of disobedience”); while the Lord came to cast down the devil, and clear the air and prepare the way for us up into heaven, as said the Apostle: “Through the veil, that is to say, His flesh”—and this must needs be by death—well, by what other kind of death could this have come to pass, than by one which took place in the air, I mean the cross? for only he that is perfected on the cross dies in the air.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Whence it was quite fitting that the Lord suffered this death.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For thus being lifted up He cleared the air of the malignity both of the devil and of demons of all kinds, as He says: “I beheld Satan as lightning fall from heaven;” and made a new opening of the way up into heaven as He says once more: “Lift up your gates, O ye princes, and be ye lift up, ye everlasting doors.”

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For it was not the Word Himself that needed an opening of the gates, being Lord of all; nor were any of His works closed to their Maker; but we it was that needed it whom He carried up by His own body.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For as He offered it to death on behalf of all, so by it He once more made ready the way up into the heavens.
